title: Hurst Exponent Regime Filter — Trend vs Mean-Reversion Classifier
summary: The Hurst exponent quantifies long-range dependence in a return series: H>0.5 indicates persistent/trending behaviour, H≈0.5 is random walk, and H<0.5 indicates anti-persistent/mean-reverting behaviour. A QuantConnect practitioner study identified this as a 'fractal regime filter' that acts as an upstream analytical gate separating exploitable directional regimes from noise. For Decifer's 9-dimension signal scorer, this matters because trend signals (trend, momentum, breakout) have positive expected IC in trending markets but negative IC in choppy markets — Hurst can route the scoring to the right sub-signals per ticker.
expected_impact: Separates trending markets (H>0.5) from mean-reverting markets (H<0.5) at the individual stock level; allows Decifer to route signals to the correct dimension (trend/momentum vs mean-reversion) based on each ticker's current behaviour rather than applying all 9 dimensions equally
